decision.rule: All IDs (DispatchId, RoundId, AgentId, SenderId, etc.) must use branded types: type Brand<K, T> = K & { readonly __brand: T }
decision.rationale: Prevents ID confusion at compile time. A DispatchId cannot be passed where an AgentId is expected.

decision.rule: No thrown exceptions crossing module boundaries. All fallible operations return Result<T, E> using Ok() and Err() constructors.
decision.rationale: Explicit error handling at every call site. Callers cannot ignore errors.

decision.rule: Database schema must separate into Runtime (3NF writes), Reporting (star schema reads), and Semantic (business metrics only). No cross-layer direct references.
decision.rationale: Write-optimized and read-optimized schemas serve different access patterns. Semantic layer provides stable business contract.

decision.rule: TypeScript must compile clean under strict mode. No 'any' types. Use 'unknown' with type guards when type is uncertain. Readonly everything.
decision.rationale: Boris Cherny strict mode catches entire classes of bugs at compile time.

decision.rule: All dispatch events, crawl events, and agent decisions must be logged to JSONL. Each line is a self-contained JSON object with timestamp.
decision.rationale: Append-only structured logging enables offline analysis and audit trails without database dependency.

decision.rule: All repos must maintain passing test suites. New code must include tests. Quality thresholds escalate across rounds (0.60 to 0.85).
decision.rationale: 386/386 tests pass across the monorepo. Regressions are blockers.

decision.rule: No hardcoded API keys, tokens, passwords, or PII in source code. Use environment variables or secrets management. URL allowlists enforced.
decision.rationale: OWASP top 10 compliance. Security scanners run in CI.

decision.rule: TypeScript: camelCase for variables/functions, PascalCase for types/classes/enums. Python: snake_case for variables/functions, PascalCase for classes. File names: kebab-case for TS, snake_case for Python.
decision.rationale: Consistent naming across the monorepo reduces cognitive load.

decision.rule: MCP servers use @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, expose tools via Zod schemas, and provide resources for documentation. Server name follows 'domain-capability' pattern.
decision.rationale: Consistent MCP server pattern enables tool discovery and 98.7% token reduction.

decision.rule: Agent definitions use YAML frontmatter in .claude/agents/*.md with fields: name, description, tools, model, memory. Body describes role, capabilities, workflow, constraints.
decision.rationale: Declarative agent definitions enable both Claude Code (CLI invocation) and Claude Cowork (sub-agent dispatch).

decision.rule: No unnecessary dependencies. Pure-Python implementations preferred over PyPI packages when feasible (e.g., Bloom filter). Lock files committed. No floating versions in production.
decision.rationale: Minimizes supply chain attack surface and avoids dependency hell (e.g., scrapy-magicfields BaseItem removal).

decision.rule: Iterative processes must generate context deltas (new_patterns, failing_targets, quality_trajectory, steer_direction) that inject into the next iteration.
decision.rationale: Prevents repetitive failures. Each iteration builds on accumulated knowledge.
